content:
What are the Federal Clean Technology Grants?

Complete guide to federal clean tech funding programs available to women-owned environmental businesses across all Canadian provinces through SDTC, NRCan, and innovation programs.

{/* SDTC Program */}
Sustainable Development Technology Canada (SDTC) - Up to $10M Clean Tech Commercialization

SDTC Funding for Women Clean Tech

Maximum Funding: Up to $10,000,000
Typical Project Size: $1M - $5M
Cost Share: Up to 40% project costs

Eligible SDTC Activities Women Entrepreneurs:

• Technology Development: Pre-commercial demonstration, pilot projects, technology validation for clean tech innovations

• Commercialization: Scale-up activities, manufacturing readiness, market entry preparation for environmental technology

• Field Trials: Real-world testing, performance validation, customer demonstrations of clean tech solutions

• IP Development: Patent filing, technology protection, licensing preparation for clean tech intellectual property

• Partnerships: Industry collaborations, research partnerships, supply chain development for commercialization

• Market Analysis: Market research, business model development, go-to-market strategy for clean technology

SDTC Success Stories - Women Clean Tech

🌱 Toronto Solar Tech - $4M SDTC Funding

Women-owned solar technology company in Toronto received SDTC funding for advanced solar panel manufacturing scale-up with 40% efficiency improvement reducing renewable energy costs commercial installations.

Location: Toronto ON | Sector: Solar Energy | Impact: 2 MW installed capacity

🌱 Vancouver Water Tech - $3.5M SDTC Grant

Women entrepreneur water treatment company in Vancouver obtained SDTC commercialization funding for advanced membrane technology treating industrial wastewater, mining effluent with 95% water recovery rate.

Location: Vancouver BC | Sector: Water Treatment | Clients: 15 industrial sites

🌱 Montreal Battery Storage - $5M SDTC Funding

Women-led energy storage technology company in Montreal secured SDTC grant for grid-scale battery system commercialization enabling renewable energy integration reducing fossil fuel dependency electricity grid.

Location: Montreal QC | Sector: Energy Storage | Capacity: 50 MWh systems

🌱 Calgary Carbon Capture - $6M SDTC Grant

Women entrepreneur carbon capture technology company in Calgary received SDTC funding for direct air capture system scale-up removing CO2 from atmosphere for permanent storage or industrial use carbon-negative solutions.

Location: Calgary AB | Sector: Carbon Capture | Removal: 1,000 tonnes CO2/year

📍 SDTC Eligible Clean Technology Sectors - Women Entrepreneurs Priority Areas

Clean Energy:

  • • Solar, wind, hydro renewable energy
  • • Energy storage and grid solutions
  • • Bioenergy and alternative fuels
  • • Energy efficiency technology

Environmental Solutions:

  • • Water treatment and conservation
  • • Air quality and emissions control
  • • Waste reduction and recycling
  • • Soil remediation technology

Climate Solutions:

  • • Carbon capture and storage
  • • Climate adaptation technology
  • • Sustainable transportation
  • • Green building materials
{/* NRCan Clean Energy Programs */}
Natural Resources Canada (NRCan) - Clean Energy Innovation & Renewable Energy Programs

NRCan Clean Energy Programs Women

NRCan Funding Programs:

  • • Energy Innovation Program: Funding for early-stage renewable energy R&D, clean energy technology development women entrepreneurs
  • • Clean Growth Program: Commercialization support for clean energy solutions reducing emissions, improving efficiency
  • • Smart Renewables: Grid integration technology, energy storage, renewable energy optimization funding
  • • Clean Fuels Program: Alternative fuels development, biofuels, hydrogen, renewable natural gas innovation
  • • Energy Efficiency: Building retrofit technology, industrial efficiency, energy management systems women cleantech

NRCan Support Services:

  • • Clean energy technical expertise and advisory
  • • Technology testing and validation facilities
  • • Market intelligence and sector analysis
  • • International partnerships and collaboration

NRCan Women Clean Energy Success

💡 Ottawa Geothermal - NRCan Innovation Funding

Women-owned geothermal energy company in Ottawa received NRCan funding for advanced geothermal heat pump technology reducing building heating costs 60% with zero emissions renewable thermal energy.

Location: Ottawa ON | Technology: Geothermal | Savings: 60% energy reduction

💡 Edmonton Biomass - NRCan Clean Fuels Grant

Women entrepreneur biomass energy company in Edmonton obtained NRCan funding for agricultural waste-to-energy technology producing renewable natural gas from farm residues reducing methane emissions.

Location: Edmonton AB | Feedstock: Agricultural waste | Output: RNG fuel

💡 Quebec City Wind Tech - NRCan Smart Renewables

Women-led wind turbine optimization company in Quebec City secured NRCan funding for AI-powered wind farm management software increasing energy output 15% through predictive maintenance and smart operations.

Location: Quebec City QC | Solution: AI Wind Optimization | Increase: +15% output

{/* Net-Zero Accelerator */}
Net-Zero Accelerator - Climate Solutions & Emissions Reduction Technology Funding

Net-Zero Accelerator Programs

Climate Technology Support:

  • • Emissions Reduction: Technology reducing greenhouse gas emissions industrial processes, transportation, buildings
  • • Decarbonization: Clean manufacturing, sustainable production, low-carbon supply chains women businesses
  • • Carbon Management: Carbon capture utilization storage (CCUS), nature-based climate solutions
  • • Clean Tech Scale-up: Commercialization support for proven climate solutions ready for market deployment

Net-Zero Priority Sectors

Heavy Industry: Steel, cement, chemicals decarbonization technology

Transportation: Electric vehicles, sustainable aviation fuels, marine electrification

Buildings: Net-zero buildings, deep energy retrofits, green construction

Agriculture: Sustainable farming, precision agriculture, regenerative practices

Forestry: Carbon sequestration, sustainable forestry, bioproducts innovation

{/* Innovation Canada Clean Tech */}
Innovation Canada Clean Tech - IRAP, NRC, Regional Development Programs

Federal Innovation Support

  • • NRC IRAP: Up to $10M for clean tech R&D, commercialization, women cleantech businesses small medium enterprises
  • • Regional Development: FedDev Ontario, PrairiesCan, PacifiCan clean tech business support women entrepreneurs
  • • Strategic Innovation Fund: Large-scale clean tech projects, transformative technology, women-led innovation
  • • Accelerators & Incubators: Cleantech accelerators supporting women entrepreneurs across Canada

Clean Tech Ecosystem Support

MaRS Cleantech: Toronto cleantech accelerator supporting women clean tech entrepreneurs

Foresight Canada: National cleantech accelerator with women entrepreneur programs

Écotech Québec: Quebec cleantech cluster supporting women environmental businesses

Innovate BC: BC cleantech programs for women sustainability entrepreneurs
shortAnswer: Yes — Complete 2026-2027 guide to clean tech grants for women entrepreneurs. SDTC funding up to $10M, NRCan clean energy programs, provincial sustainability grants Toronto Vancouver Montreal Calgary.. Funding available: up to $10M.
shortAnswerQuestion: What grants are available for women-owned businesses in 2026?

faq.question: What is the application deadline for Women Clean Technology Grants Canada -2027 | Clean Energy Funding , Sustainability Innovation Support Toronto Vancouver Montreal in 2026?
faq.answer: Deadlines typically vary by specific program tier. However, for 2026, many initiatives operate on a rolling intake basis until funds are depleted. Check the official portal for specific cutoffs.

faq.question: How much funding can I get through Women Clean Technology Grants Canada -2027 | Clean Energy Funding , Sustainability Innovation Support Toronto Vancouver Montreal?
faq.answer: Funding amounts range depending on business size and scope. Top awards can reach $10M.

faq.question: Do I have to give up equity for this program?
faq.answer: No. Government grants and subsidies are non-dilutive, meaning you retain 100% ownership of your company.

expertTip.title: Dual Diversity Priority
expertTip.content: Women-led clean technology businesses receive priority scoring in SDTC applications because they meet both diversity and environmental innovation mandates.
